assertions.text: Correctly classifies the chatbot as limited risk (not high-risk) under Art. 50 — not falling under Annex III
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'RISK TIER CONCLUSION: Your e-commerce chatbot is LIMITED RISK under Art. 50. It is NOT high-risk. It does not fall into any of the eight Annex III high-risk use case areas'

assertions.text: Correctly states Art. 50(1) disclosure obligation: deployers must inform users they interact with an AI system
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly cites 'Art. 50(1) — Mandatory AI Disclosure: deployers of chatbots must inform users they are interacting with an AI system' as the primary obligation

assertions.text: Notes that an e-commerce chatbot is not 'obviously AI' to a reasonably well-informed person and therefore disclosure is required
assertions.evidence: Response states 'Art. 50(1) is unambiguous: A customer chatbot on an e-commerce site does not meet the obvious exception — disclosure is mandatory' and that the 'obvious from context' exception does not apply

assertions.text: Distinguishes deployer obligations (Art. 26, Art. 50) from provider/GPAI provider obligations (Art. 53)
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly distinguishes: 'No High-Risk Obligations (Arts. 9-17 and 26) do not apply' as deployer vs. 'Your third-party API provider bears GPAI obligations under Arts. 53-55'

assertions.text: States the 2 August 2026 application date for Art. 50 limited-risk transparency requirements
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'ART. 50(1) — MANDATORY AI DISCLOSURE (applies from 2 August 2026)'
